title: Contact Old Age Security - Canada.ca
content:
Information about the Old Age Security program
Save time by using these online services, available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week.

### Check the status of your application and manage your information online in My Service Canada Account
My Service Canada Account (MSCA) allows you to:
- access your Old Age Security (OAS) benefits application status
- access your payment information
- change your telephone number
- print tax slips and manage your income tax deductions
- authorize someone to communicate with Service Canada on your behalf
- submit supporting documentation online
Register or sign in to MSCA

### Apply online for OAS pensions
Find out if you qualify and how to apply for:
- Old Age Security (OAS) pension
- Guaranteed Income Supplement (GIS)
- Guaranteed Income Supplement - Allowance
- Allowance for the Survivor
For any OAS -related questions or changes to  your direct deposit and contact information , call us.
When you call, you may need:
- Social Insurance Number (SIN)
Canada and the United States (operates in local time):
Outside of Canada and the United States (operates in Eastern Time):
1-613-957-1954 (collect)
Canada VRS (Video Relay Service):
- download the Canada Video Relay Service (VRS) app
- once you are registered, you can find our number in the VRS directory
- call us through the app
If you use any other relay service, call our toll-free number (1-800-277-9914) instead.
8:30 am to 4:30 pm Automated service available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
Public
          holidays

This is the average time callers waited before reaching an officer. Our service standard is to answer your call within 10 minutes.

You can send your documents by mail.
If you reside outside of Canada, mail your documents to a Service Canada Office in the province where you last resided .

#### Provincial mailing address
Province and territory
Newfoundland and Labrador
Service Canada PO Box 9430 Station A St. John's NL A1A 2Y5 CANADA
Prince Edward Island
Service Canada PO Box 8000 Station Central Charlottetown PE C1A 8K1 CANADA For disability applications only: Service Canada PO Box 1687 Station Central Halifax NS B3J 3J4 CANADA
Service Canada PO Box 1687 Station Central Halifax NS B3J 3J4 CANADA
Service Canada PO Box 250 Station A Fredericton NB E3B 4Z6 CANADA
Service Canada PO Box 1816 Station Terminus Quebec QC G1K 7L5 CANADA For Canada Pension Plan and Disability applications mail to: Service Canada PO Box 250 Station A Fredericton NB E3B 4Z6 CANADA
Ontario For postal codes beginning with "L, M or N"
Service Canada PO Box 5100 Station D Scarborough ON M1R 5C8 CANADA
Ontario For postal codes beginning with "K or P"
Service Canada PO Box 2013 Station Main Timmins ON P4N 8C8 CANADA For Disability applications only Service Canada PO Box 2020 Station Main Chatham ON N7M 6B2 CANADA
Manitoba and Saskatchewan
Service Canada PO Box 818 Station Main Winnipeg MB R3C 2N4 CANADA
Alberta/Northwest Territories and Nunavut
For Old Age Security and Disability applications Service Canada PO Box 2710 Station Main Edmonton AB T5J 2G4 CANADA For Canada Pension Plan applications mail to: Service Canada PO Box 818 Station Main Winnipeg MB R3C 2N4 CANADA
British Columbia and Yukon
Service Canada PO Box 1177 Station CSC Victoria BC V8W 2V2 CANADA

### Before you come, you may need:
- a valid ID: the ID must show your current legal name, photo, signature and/or date of birth

#### Examples of valid ID
- One of the following: a driver's license, or a provincial or territorial ID card a Canadian or foreign passport, or permanent resident card a Certificate of Indian Status issued by the Government of Canada
- One of the following: a driver's license, or a provincial or territorial ID card a Canadian or foreign passport, or permanent resident card a Certificate of Indian Status issued by the Government of Canada
- a driver's license, or a provincial or territorial ID card
- a Canadian or foreign passport, or permanent resident card
- a Certificate of Indian Status issued by the Government of Canada
- Two of the following: a provincial or territorial birth certificate a certificate of Canadian citizenship a certificate of registration of birth abroad any other Canadian government-issued ID
- Two of the following: a provincial or territorial birth certificate a certificate of Canadian citizenship a certificate of registration of birth abroad any other Canadian government-issued ID
- a provincial or territorial birth certificate
- a certificate of Canadian citizenship
- a certificate of registration of birth abroad
- any other Canadian government-issued ID
Find a Service Canada Office

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
